key_points: The datacenter CPU market is fundamentally shifting due to Reinforcement Learning and Agentic AI workloads.
DRAM pricing and supply shortages are actively constraining future CPU roadmaps.
Next-generation CPU architectures (e.g., AMD Venice, Intel Diamond Rapids) require new interconnect paradigms to survive these bottlenecks.

issues.description: “arrival”被翻译为“问世”。考虑到AI训练与推理技术实际上已经存在了数十年，只是在近期才大规模进入公众和算力市场的视野，使用“问世”（通常指某物首次被发明或创造出来）略显不妥。
翻译保留了英文拼写“Kunpeng”。虽然Prompt指引中提到尽量保留代号英文，但由于“鲲鹏”是华为的本土品牌，且在中文科技语境中极少使用拼音形式，直接使用中文更能拉近与中文读者的距离。

paragraphs.source: Since 2023, the datacenter story has been simple. GPUs and networking are king. The arrival and subsequent explosion of AI Training and Inference have shifted compute demands away from the CPU. This meant that Intel, the primary supplier of server CPUs, failed to ride the wave of datacenter buildout and spending. Server CPU revenue remained relatively stagnant as hyperscalers and neoclouds focused on GPUs and datacenter infrastructure.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Since 2023, the datacenter story has been simple. GPUs and networking are king. The arrival and subsequent explosion of AI Training and Inference have shifted compute demands away from the CPU. This meant that Intel, the primary supplier of server CPUs, failed to ride the wave of datacenter buildout and spending. Server CPU revenue remained relatively stagnant as hyperscalers and neoclouds focused on GPUs and datacenter infrastructure.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Since 2023, the datacenter story has been simple. GPUs and networking are king. The arrival and subsequent explosion of AI Training and Inference have shifted compute demands away from the CPU. This meant that Intel, the primary supplier of server CPUs, failed to ride the wave of datacenter buildout and spending. Server CPU revenue remained relatively stagnant as hyperscalers and neoclouds focused on GPUs and datacenter infrastructure.

pro.text: 自 2023 年以来，数据中心的叙事逻辑极其简单：GPU 与网络设备称王。AI 训练与推理的问世及随后的爆发，抽离了原本属于 CPU 的计算需求。这导致服务器 CPU 的主要供应商英特尔，未能乘上这波数据中心建设与支出狂潮的东风。随着超大规模云厂商与新锐云厂商将重心转向 GPU 及数据中心基础设施，服务器 CPU 营收一直停滞不前。

paragraphs.source: At the same time, the same hyperscalers have been rolling their own ARM-based datacenter CPUs for their cloud computing services, closing off a significant addressable market for Intel. And within their own x86 turf, Intel’s lackluster execution and uncompetitive performance to rival AMD has further eroded market share. Without a competent AI accelerator offering, Intel was left to tread water while the rest of the industry feasted.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: At the same time, the same hyperscalers have been rolling their own ARM-based datacenter CPUs for their cloud computing services, closing off a significant addressable market for Intel. And within their own x86 turf, Intel’s lackluster execution and uncompetitive performance to rival AMD has further eroded market share. Without a competent AI accelerator offering, Intel was left to tread water while the rest of the industry feasted.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: At the same time, the same hyperscalers have been rolling their own ARM-based datacenter CPUs for their cloud computing services, closing off a significant addressable market for Intel. And within their own x86 turf, Intel’s lackluster execution and uncompetitive performance to rival AMD has further eroded market share. Without a competent AI accelerator offering, Intel was left to tread water while the rest of the industry feasted.

pro.text: 与此同时，这些超大规模云厂商一直在为其云计算服务自研基于 ARM 架构的数据中心 CPU，直接封死了英特尔一大片可寻址市场。而在自家的 x86 阵地内，英特尔拉垮的执行力，加上面对竞争对手 AMD 时毫无竞争力的性能表现，进一步蚕食了其市场份额。由于拿不出能打的 AI 加速器产品，当整个行业在尽情狂欢时，英特尔只能在原地停滞不前。

paragraphs.source: Over the last 6 months this has changed massively. We have posted multiple reports to Core Research and the Tokenomics Model about soaring CPU demand. The primary drivers we have shown and modeled are reinforcement learning and vibe coding’s incredible demand on CPUs. We have also covered major CPU cloud deals by multiple vendors with AI labs. We also have modeling of how many CPUs of what types are being deployed.

inline_elements.text: Core Research
Tokenomics Model

paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Over the last 6 months this has changed massively. We have posted multiple reports to [Core Research](https://semianalysis.com/core-research/) and the [Tokenomics Model](https://semianalysis.com/tokenomics-model/) about soaring CPU demand. The primary drivers we have shown and modeled are reinforcement learning and vibe coding’s incredible demand on CPUs. We have also covered major CPU cloud deals by multiple vendors with AI labs. We also have modeling of how many CPUs of what types are being deployed.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Over the last 6 months this has changed massively. We have posted multiple reports to Core Research and the Tokenomics Model about soaring CPU demand. The primary drivers we have shown and modeled are reinforcement learning and vibe coding’s incredible demand on CPUs. We have also covered major CPU cloud deals by multiple vendors with AI labs. We also have modeling of how many CPUs of what types are being deployed.

parent_inline_elements.text: Core Research
Tokenomics Model

pro.text: 过去六个月里，情况发生了翻天覆地的变化。我们在 Core Research 与 Tokenomics Model 中发布了多份关于 CPU 需求飙升的报告。我们展示并建模分析的主要驱动力，是强化学习 (RL) 与直觉编程 (vibe coding) 对 CPU 产生的惊人需求。我们还报道了多家供应商与 AI 实验室达成的大型 CPU 云服务协议，并建模分析了正在部署的 CPU 数量及类型。

paragraphs.source: However, Intel’s recent rallies and changing demand signals in the latter part of 2025 have shown that CPUs are now relevant again. In their latest Q4 earnings, Intel saw an unexpected uptick in datacenter CPU demand in late 2025 and are increasing 2026 capex guidance on foundry tools and prioritizing wafers to server from PC to alleviate supply constraints in serving this new demand. This marks an inflection point in the role of CPUs in the datacenter, with AI model training and inference using CPUs more intensively.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: However, Intel’s recent rallies and changing demand signals in the latter part of 2025 have shown that CPUs are now relevant again. In their latest Q4 earnings, Intel saw an unexpected uptick in datacenter CPU demand in late 2025 and are increasing 2026 capex guidance on foundry tools and prioritizing wafers to server from PC to alleviate supply constraints in serving this new demand. This marks an inflection point in the role of CPUs in the datacenter, with AI model training and inference using CPUs more intensively.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: However, Intel’s recent rallies and changing demand signals in the latter part of 2025 have shown that CPUs are now relevant again. In their latest Q4 earnings, Intel saw an unexpected uptick in datacenter CPU demand in late 2025 and are increasing 2026 capex guidance on foundry tools and prioritizing wafers to server from PC to alleviate supply constraints in serving this new demand. This marks an inflection point in the role of CPUs in the datacenter, with AI model training and inference using CPUs more intensively.

pro.text: 然而，英特尔近期的强势反弹以及 2025 年下半年需求信号的转变表明，CPU 如今已重回舞台中心。在最新的第四季度财报中，英特尔发现 2025 年底数据中心 CPU 需求出现意外上扬。为此，他们正在上调 2026 年晶圆代工厂（foundry）设备的资本支出（capex）指引，并将晶圆产能分配从 PC 优先向服务器倾斜，以缓解满足这一新增需求时的供应链瓶颈。这标志着 CPU 在数据中心的角色迎来拐点，AI 模型训练与推理对 CPU 的依赖愈发显著。

paragraphs.source: 2026 is an exciting year for the datacenter CPU, with many new generations launching this year from all vendors amid the boom in demand. As such, this piece serves to paint the CPU landscape in 2026. We lay the groundwork, covering the history of the datacenter CPU and the evolving demand drivers, with deep dives on datacenter CPU architecture changes from Intel and AMD over the years.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: 2026 is an exciting year for the datacenter CPU, with many new generations launching this year from all vendors amid the boom in demand. paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: 2026 is an exciting year for the datacenter CPU, with many new generations launching this year from all vendors amid the boom in demand. As such, this piece serves to paint the CPU landscape in 2026. We lay the groundwork, covering the history of the datacenter CPU and the evolving demand drivers, with deep dives on datacenter CPU architecture changes from Intel and AMD over the years.

pro.text: 2026 年是数据中心 CPU 令人振奋的一年。在需求激增的背景下，各大供应商都将在今年推出众多新一代产品。因此，本文将为您描绘 2026 年的 CPU 市场格局。我们将首先铺垫背景，回顾数据中心 CPU 的发展史以及不断演变的需求驱动因素，并深入剖析英特尔与 AMD 多年来在数据中心 CPU 架构上的变迁。

pro.text: 接下来，我们将详细阐述 ARM 阵营的竞争态势。这包括英伟达（NVIDIA）的 Grace 与 Vera 架构、亚马逊的 Graviton 系列、微软的 Cobalt、谷歌的 Axion CPU 系列、Ampere Computing 进军商用 ARM 芯片市场的举措及其被软银收购的事件、ARM 自家的 Phoenix CPU 设计，并审视华为自主研发的 Kunpeng CPU 进展。

paragraphs.source: For our subscribers, we provide our datacenter CPU roadmap to 2028 and detail the datacenter CPUs beyond 2026 from AMD, Intel, ARM and Qualcomm. We then look ahead to what the future looks like for datacenter CPUs, discuss the effects of the DRAM shortage, what NVIDIA’s Bluefield-4 Context Memory Storage platform means for the future of general purpose CPUs, and the key trends to look out for in the CPU market and CPU designs going forward.

pro.text: 面向我们的订阅用户，我们将提供直至 2028 年的数据中心 CPU 路线图，并详细介绍 AMD、英特尔、ARM 与高通（Qualcomm）在 2026 年之后的数据中心 CPU 规划。随后，我们将展望数据中心 CPU 的未来图景，探讨 DRAM 短缺带来的影响，分析英伟达 Bluefield-4 的上下文内存存储平台对通用 CPU 未来的意义，并指出未来 CPU 市场与设计中值得关注的关键趋势。

key_points: The datacenter CPU market is fundamentally shifting due to Reinforcement Learning and Agentic AI workloads.
DRAM pricing and supply shortages are actively constraining future CPU roadmaps.
Next-generation CPU architectures (e.g., AMD Venice, Intel Diamond Rapids) require new interconnect paradigms to survive these bottlenecks.
translation_notes: Ensure codenames (Venice, Verano, Florence, Diamond Rapids, Coral Rapids, Phoenix, Venom) are kept in English or use standard industry transliterations.
Maintain the premium, insider tone of the paywall teaser ('For our subscribers...').
Translate 'Context Memory Storage' precisely, as it is a specific technical paradigm introduced by NVIDIA's Bluefield-4.

issues.description: “arrival”被翻译为“问世”。考虑到AI训练与推理技术实际上已经存在了数十年，只是在近期才大规模进入公众和算力市场的视野，使用“问世”（通常指某物首次被发明或创造出来）略显不妥。
翻译保留了英文拼写“Kunpeng”。虽然Prompt指引中提到尽量保留代号英文，但由于“鲲鹏”是华为的本土品牌，且在中文科技语境中极少使用拼音形式，直接使用中文更能拉近与中文读者的距离。

paragraphs.source: The modern version of the datacenter CPU can be traced back to the 1990s following the success of Personal Computers in the prior decade, bringing basic computing into the home. As PC processing power grew with Intel’s i386, i486 and Pentium generations, many tasks normally computed by advanced workstation and mainframe computers from the likes of DEC and IBM were instead done on PCs at a fraction of the cost. Responding to this need for higher performance “mainframe replacements”, Intel began to release PC processor variants that had more performance and larger caches for higher prices, starting with the Pentium Pro in 1995 that had multiple L2 cache dies co-packaged with the CPU in a Multi-Chip Module (MCM).
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The modern version of the datacenter CPU can be traced back to the 1990s following the success of Personal Computers in the prior decade, bringing basic computing into the home. As PC processing power grew with Intel’s i386, i486 and Pentium generations, many tasks normally computed by advanced workstation and mainframe computers from the likes of DEC and IBM were instead done on PCs at a fraction of the cost. Responding to this need for higher performance “mainframe replacements”, Intel began to release PC processor variants that had more performance and larger caches for higher prices, starting with the Pentium Pro in 1995 that had multiple L2 cache dies co-packaged with the CPU in a Multi-Chip Module (MCM). The Xeon brand then followed suit in 1998, with the Pentium II Xeons that similarly had multiple L2 cache dies added to the CPU processor slot. While mainframes still continue today in the IBM Z lines used for bank transaction verifications and such, they remain a niche corner of the market that we will not cover in this piece.

The Xeon brand then followed suit in 1998, with the Pentium II Xeons that similarly had multiple L2 cache dies added to the CPU processor slot. While mainframes still continue today in the IBM Z lines used for bank transaction verifications and such, they remain a niche corner of the market that we will not cover in this piece.

pro.text: 现代数据中心 CPU 的雏形可追溯至 20 世纪 90 年代。在此前十年间，个人电脑大获成功，让基础计算真正走入千家万户。随着英特尔 i386、i486 和奔腾系列产品的迭代，PC 算力水涨船高。许多原本依赖 DEC 和 IBM 等高级工作站与大型机处理的任务转由 PC 承担，成本仅为前者的零头。面对市场对高性能“大型机替代品”的渴求，英特尔开始推出性能更强、缓存更大的高价位 PC 处理器变体。这一趋势始于 1995 年问世的奔腾 Pro，该处理器采用多芯片模块 (MCM) 技术，将多个 L2缓存裸片与 CPU 共封装。

paragraphs.source: The 2000s brought the internet age, with the emergence of Web 2.0, e-mail, e-commerce, Google search, smartphones with 3G broadband data, and the need for datacenter CPUs to serve the world’s internet traffic as everything went online. Datacenter CPUs grew into a multi-billion dollar segment. On the design front, after the GHz wars were over with the end of Dennard scaling, attention shifted to multi-core CPUs and increased integration. AMD integrated the memory controller into the CPU silicon, and high-speed IO (PCIe) came directly from the CPU as well. Multi-core CPUs were especially suited for datacenter workloads, where many tasks could be run in parallel across different cores.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The 2000s brought the internet age, with the emergence of Web 2.0, e-mail, e-commerce, Google search, smartphones with 3G broadband data, and the need for datacenter CPUs to serve the world’s internet traffic as everything went online. Datacenter CPUs grew into a multi-billion dollar segment. On the design front, after the GHz wars were over with the end of Dennard scaling, attention shifted to multi-core CPUs and increased integration. AMD integrated the memory controller into the CPU silicon, and high-speed IO (PCIe) came directly from the CPU as well. Multi-core CPUs were especially suited for datacenter workloads, where many tasks could be run in parallel across different cores.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The 2000s brought the internet age, with the emergence of Web 2.0, e-mail, e-commerce, Google search, smartphones with 3G broadband data, and the need for datacenter CPUs to serve the world’s internet traffic as everything went online. Datacenter CPUs grew into a multi-billion dollar segment. On the design front, after the GHz wars were over with the end of Dennard scaling, attention shifted to multi-core CPUs and increased integration. AMD integrated the memory controller into the CPU silicon, and high-speed IO (PCIe) came directly from the CPU as well. Multi-core CPUs were especially suited for datacenter workloads, where many tasks could be run in parallel across different cores.

pro.text: 21 世纪初迎来了互联网时代。Web 2.0、电子邮件、电子商务、谷歌搜索以及支持 3G 宽带数据的智能手机相继涌现。随着万物触网，数据中心 CPU 必须承担起处理全球互联网流量的重任，并随之成长为一个价值数十亿美元的细分市场。在设计层面，随着登纳德缩放定律走向终结，“主频大战”偃旗息鼓，业界的注意力转向了多核 CPU 与更高的集成度。AMD 将内存控制器直接集成到 CPU 裸片中，高速 I/O（PCIe）也改为直接由 CPU 提供。多核 CPU 格外契合数据中心工作负载，能让大量任务在不同核心上并行处理。

applied_terms: Translate 'Dennard scaling' as '登纳德缩放定律'.
Ensure 'GHz wars' reflects the historical context (主频大战).
Use standard industry acronyms alongside their Chinese equivalents for 'SMT' (同步多线程) and 'QPI' (快速通道互联).

understanding.role_in_article: Explains the architectural pivot from frequency scaling to multi-core designs driven by the internet boom and physical semiconductor limits.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Shows how the foundational PC-era architectures had to adapt to the massive traffic scaling of Web 2.0.
understanding.relation_to_next: Sets the stage for the virtualization era, as having multiple cores and threads enabled the partitioning of physical servers into virtual ones.
key_points: The end of Dennard scaling forced the industry to abandon frequency increases (GHz wars) in favor of multi-core CPU designs.
Simultaneous Multi-Threading (SMT) and integrated memory controllers were introduced to maximize throughput for parallelizable internet workloads.
Multi-socket servers (using QPI) became necessary to meet the insatiable demand for compute.
translation_notes: Translate 'Dennard scaling' as '登纳德缩放定律'.
Ensure 'GHz wars' reflects the historical context (主频大战).
Use standard industry acronyms alongside their Chinese equivalents for 'SMT' (同步多线程) and 'QPI' (快速通道互联).

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。
前文将“Multi-socket”翻译为行业标准的“多路”，但后文将“sockets”翻译为“插槽”。

paragraphs.source: We will detail the evolution of how these multiple cores are connected in the interconnect section below. Simultaneous Multi-Threading (SMT) was also introduced in this time by both AMD and Intel, partitioning a core into two logical threads that could operate independently while sharing most core resources, further improving performance in parallelizable datacenter workloads. Those looking for more performance would turn to Multi-socket CPU servers, with Intel’s Quick Path Interconnect (QPI) and AMD’s HyperTransport Direct Connect Architecture in their Opteron CPUs providing coherent links between up to eight sockets per server.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: We will detail the evolution of how these multiple cores are connected in the interconnect section below. Simultaneous Multi-Threading (SMT) was also introduced in this time by both AMD and Intel, partitioning a core into two logical threads that could operate independently while sharing most core resources, further improving performance in parallelizable datacenter workloads. Those looking for more performance would turn to Multi-socket CPU servers, with Intel’s Quick Path Interconnect (QPI) and AMD’s HyperTransport Direct Connect Architecture in their Opteron CPUs providing coherent links between up to eight sockets per server.

Simultaneous Multi-Threading (SMT) was also introduced in this time by both AMD and Intel, partitioning a core into two logical threads that could operate independently while sharing most core resources, further improving performance in parallelizable datacenter workloads. Those looking for more performance would turn to Multi-socket CPU servers, with Intel’s Quick Path Interconnect (QPI) and AMD’s HyperTransport Direct Connect Architecture in their Opteron CPUs providing coherent links between up to eight sockets per server.

pro.text: 我们将在下文的互连架构章节详细剖析多核连接方式的演进历程。AMD 和英特尔也在这一时期引入了同步多线程 (SMT) 技术。该技术将一个物理核心划分为两个逻辑线程，两者在共享大部分核心资源的同时能够独立运行，从而进一步提升了可并行化数据中心工作负载的性能。对性能有更高要求的用户则会转向多路 CPU 服务器。英特尔的快速通道互联 (QPI) 与 AMD 皓龙 CPU 采用的 HyperTransport直连架构，能够在单台服务器中为多达八个插槽提供一致性链路。

sections.title: The Virtualization and Cloud Computing Hyperscaler Era
sections.title_translation: 虚拟化与超大规模云计算时代

paragraphs.source: The next major inflection point came with cloud computing in the late 2000s, and was the primary growth driver for datacenter CPU sales throughout the 2010s. Much like how GPU Neoclouds are operating today, computing resources began consolidating toward public cloud providers and hyperscalers such as Amazon’s Web Services (AWS) as customers traded CapEx for OpEx. Spurred by the effects of the Great Recession, many enterprises could not afford to buy and run their own servers to run their software and services.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The next major inflection point came with cloud computing in the late 2000s, and was the primary growth driver for datacenter CPU sales throughout the 2010s. Much like how GPU Neoclouds are operating today, computing resources began consolidating toward public cloud providers and hyperscalers such as Amazon’s Web Services (AWS) as customers traded CapEx for OpEx. Spurred by the effects of the Great Recession, many enterprises could not afford to buy and run their own servers to run their software and services.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The next major inflection point came with cloud computing in the late 2000s, and was the primary growth driver for datacenter CPU sales throughout the 2010s. Much like how GPU Neoclouds are operating today, computing resources began consolidating toward public cloud providers and hyperscalers such as Amazon’s Web Services (AWS) as customers traded CapEx for OpEx. Spurred by the effects of the Great Recession, many enterprises could not afford to buy and run their own servers to run their software and services.

pro.text: 下一个重大转折点出现在 2000 年代末，即云计算的兴起。这也是整个 2010 年代数据中心 CPU 销量增长的核心驱动力。正如如今 GPU 新锐云厂商的运营模式一样，随着客户将资本支出转化为运营支出（opex），计算资源开始向公有云提供商以及诸如 AWS 等超大规模云服务商集中。受经济大衰退的余波冲击，许多企业已无力购买并维护自有服务器来运行其软件和服务。

applied_terms: Translate 'traded CapEx for OpEx' cleanly (将资本支出转化为运营支出).
Use 'Hyperscalers' (超大规模云服务商) consistently.
Carefully manage the tone regarding Intel's 'untimely design decisions' to maintain objective, analytical framing.

understanding.role_in_article: Details the hyperscaler revolution and the business shift from CapEx to OpEx, while highlighting critical architectural vulnerabilities that impacted performance.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Evolves the multi-core dot-com servers into consolidated, virtualized resource pools managed by hyperscalers.
understanding.relation_to_next: Contrasts the CPU-dominant cloud era with the impending AI era where GPUs challenge CPU dominance.
key_points: Cloud computing consolidated resources toward hyperscalers, fundamentally changing server buying patterns from enterprise CapEx to OpEx.
Security flaws and the resulting patches severely degraded SMT performance, haunting Intel's future architectural decisions.
translation_notes: Translate 'traded CapEx for OpEx' cleanly (将资本支出转化为运营支出).
Use 'Hyperscalers' (超大规模云服务商) consistently.
Carefully manage the tone regarding Intel's 'untimely design decisions' to maintain objective, analytical framing.

issues.description: 译文“将资本支出转化为运营支出（opex）”中保留了小写的英文缩写 opex。由于前半句的 CapEx 并未加注英文，仅对 OpEx 加注显得不对称，且“运营支出”已是标准且清晰的译法，无需额外加注。
译文“被幽灵 (漏洞) 与熔断 (漏洞) 所利用”中，使用括号单切“漏洞”二字在中文排版上显得非常生硬。对于这种业内著名的专有名词，首现时更适合保留英文原名对照，并把“漏洞”作为后缀统称。
译文“在程序运行前提前猜测”中，“运行前”与“提前”存在轻微的语义重复。

paragraphs.source: Cloud computing offered a far more palatable “pay as you use” business model with renting compute instances and running your workloads on 3rd-party hardware, which allowed spending to dynamically adjust with usage that varied over time. This scalability was more favorable than procuring one’s own servers, which needed to be utilized fully at all times to maximize ROI. The Cloud also enabled more streamlined services to emerge, such as serverless computing from the likes of AWS Lambda that automatically allocates software to computing resources, sparing the customer from having to decide on the appropriate number of instances to spin up before running a particular task. With nearly everything handled by them behind the scenes, Clouds turned compute into a commodity.

pro.text: 云计算提供了一种更易于接受的“按量付费”商业模式。通过租用计算实例并在第三方硬件上运行工作负载，客户支出可随使用量变化而动态调整。这种可扩展性远优于自行采购服务器，因为自有服务器必须时刻保持满负荷运转，才能实现投资回报率 (ROI) 最大化。云技术还催生了更高效便捷的服务，例如 AWS Lambda 等提供的无服务器计算。这类服务能自动为软件分配计算资源，客户在运行特定任务前，无需再费心决定启动多少个实例。由于云厂商在后台包揽了几乎所有工作，计算能力彻底变成了一种商品。

paragraphs.source: The key feature for a secure and resource efficient Cloud to work at all is CPU hardware virtualization. In essence, virtualization allows a single CPU to run multiple independent and secure instances of Virtual Machines (VMs) orchestrated through hypervisors such as VMware ESXi. Multi-core CPUs could be partitioned such that each VM would be assigned to a single core or logical thread, with the hypervisor able to migrate instances onto different cores, sockets or servers over the network to optimize for CPU utilization while keeping data and instructions secured from other instances operating on the same CPU.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The key feature for a secure and resource efficient Cloud to work at all is CPU hardware virtualization. In essence, virtualization allows a single CPU to run multiple independent and secure instances of Virtual Machines (VMs) orchestrated through hypervisors such as VMware ESXi. Multi-core CPUs could be partitioned such that each VM would be assigned to a single core or logical thread, with the hypervisor able to migrate instances onto different cores, sockets or servers over the network to optimize for CPU utilization while keeping data and instructions secured from other instances operating on the same CPU.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The key feature for a secure and resource efficient Cloud to work at all is CPU hardware virtualization. In essence, virtualization allows a single CPU to run multiple independent and secure instances of Virtual Machines (VMs) orchestrated through hypervisors such as VMware ESXi. Multi-core CPUs could be partitioned such that each VM would be assigned to a single core or logical thread, with the hypervisor able to migrate instances onto different cores, sockets or servers over the network to optimize for CPU utilization while keeping data and instructions secured from other instances operating on the same CPU.

pro.text: 构建安全且资源高效的云环境，其核心基石在于 CPU 硬件虚拟化。本质上，虚拟化允许单个 CPU 运行多个独立且安全的虚拟机 (VM) 实例，这些实例由 VMware ESXi 等虚拟机管理程序进行统一调度。多核 CPU 可被灵活划分，将每个虚拟机分配至单个核心或逻辑线程。虚拟机管理程序还能通过网络将实例迁移至不同的核心、插槽或服务器上，以此优化 CPU 利用率，同时确保数据与指令安全，免受同一 CPU 上其他运行实例的干扰。

paragraphs.source: The need for virtualization for the cloud, combined with CPU designers implementing SMT to boost performance was eventually exploited with the Spectre and Meltdown vulnerabilities in 2018. When two instances ran on threads running on the same physical core, it was possible for an attacker to snoop and piece together data from the other thread using the CPU cores branch prediction functions, a performance boosting technique that guessed, fetched and executed instructions ahead of the running program to keep the CPU busy. With security in the cloud potentially compromised, providers rushed to disable SMT to stop the attack vector.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The need for virtualization for the cloud, combined with CPU designers implementing SMT to boost performance was eventually exploited with the Spectre and Meltdown vulnerabilities in 2018. When two instances ran on threads running on the same physical core, it was possible for an attacker to snoop and piece together data from the other thread using the CPU cores branch prediction functions, a performance boosting technique that guessed, fetched and executed instructions ahead of the running program to keep the CPU busy. With security in the cloud potentially compromised, providers rushed to disable SMT to stop the attack vector. Despite patches and hardware fixes, the performance loss of up to 30% without SMT would haunt Intel and show up in untimely design decisions down the road which we detail below.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The need for virtualization for the cloud, combined with CPU designers implementing SMT to boost performance was eventually exploited with the Spectre and Meltdown vulnerabilities in 2018. When two instances ran on threads running on the same physical core, it was possible for an attacker to snoop and piece together data from the other thread using the CPU cores branch prediction functions, a performance boosting technique that guessed, fetched and executed instructions ahead of the running program to keep the CPU busy. With security in the cloud potentially compromised, providers rushed to disable SMT to stop the attack vector. Despite patches and hardware fixes, the performance loss of up to 30% without SMT would haunt Intel and show up in untimely design decisions down the road which we detail below.

pro.text: 云计算对虚拟化的需求，叠加 CPU 设计者为提升性能而引入的 SMT 技术，最终在 2018 年被幽灵 (漏洞) 与熔断 (漏洞) 所利用。当两个实例在同一物理核心的不同线程上运行时，攻击者可利用 CPU 核心的分支预测功能，窥探并拼凑出另一线程的数据。分支预测本是一项性能提升技术，通过在程序运行前提前猜测、提取并执行指令，使 CPU 保持满载状态。面对云端安全可能沦陷的危机，各家提供商紧急禁用 SMT，以彻底切断这一攻击路径。

pro.text: 尽管后续推出了补丁与硬件修复方案，但禁用 SMT 导致了高达 30% 的性能损失。这一代价成为英特尔挥之不去的阴影，并直接导致其在后续作出了一系列不合时宜的设计决策，我们将在下文对此展开详述。

sections.title: The AI GPU and CPU Consolidation Era

paragraphs.source: Following the COVID boom that boosted internet traffic with way more Zoom calls, e-commerce and more time spent online, datacenter CPU growth was at an all-time high. In the five years leading up to ChatGPT’s launch in November 2022, Intel shipped over 100 Million Xeon Scalable CPUs to cloud and enterprise datacenters.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Following the COVID boom that boosted internet traffic with way more Zoom calls, e-commerce and more time spent online, datacenter CPU growth was at an all-time high. In the five years leading up to ChatGPT’s launch in November 2022, Intel shipped over 100 Million Xeon Scalable CPUs to cloud and enterprise datacenters.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Following the COVID boom that boosted internet traffic with way more Zoom calls, e-commerce and more time spent online, datacenter CPU growth was at an all-time high. In the five years leading up to ChatGPT’s launch in November 2022, Intel shipped over 100 Million Xeon Scalable CPUs to cloud and enterprise datacenters.

pro.text: 新冠疫情引发了一波线上红利，Zoom 视频会议激增、电商繁荣以及网民在线时长的大幅增加，共同推高了互联网流量，数据中心 CPU 的出货量也随之创下历史新高。在 2022 年 11 月 ChatGPT 问世前的五年间，英特尔向云厂商和企业级数据中心累计出货了超 1 亿颗至强可扩展 CPU。

applied_terms: Translate 'Cloud-native CPUs' as '云原生CPU'.
Ensure 'area and power efficient' is accurately rendered (面积与能效比高).
Preserve vendor-specific core branding carefully (Atom, Zen4c/Bergamo, Altra).

understanding.role_in_article: Describes the peak of traditional CPU shipments and the industry's pivot toward highly efficient, cloud-native CPUs amidst the rise of AI accelerators.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Shows the peak of the cloud era (COVID boom) and the sudden architectural shift triggered by generative AI (ChatGPT).
understanding.relation_to_next: Transitions from the initial LLM AI boom into the specific, CPU-heavy demands of modern Reinforcement Learning.
key_points: Despite record CPU shipments leading up to the ChatGPT launch, the market is bifurcating into AI accelerators and cloud-native CPUs.
Cloud-native CPUs (e.g., Sierra Forest, Bergamo, Graviton) sacrifice cache and IO for maximum area and power efficiency via medium-sized cores.
translation_notes: Translate 'Cloud-native CPUs' as '云原生CPU'.
Ensure 'area and power efficient' is accurately rendered (面积与能效比高).
Preserve vendor-specific core branding carefully (Atom, Zen4c/Bergamo, Altra).

issues.description: 原文的“growth”指的是“增长（率/量）”，译文将其替换为“出货量”，改变了原文侧重描述增速或增量的本意。
“翻倍的 AVX-512 端口”带有一定的翻译腔，中文中“翻倍”通常作动词，较少直接作前置定语修饰名词。

paragraphs.source: From then on, AI model training and inference serving would upend the CPU’s role in the datacenter, causing widespread changes in CPU deployment and design strategies. Computing AI models requires lots of matrix multiplication, an operation that can be easily parallelized and done at massive scales on GPUs which had large arrays of vector units originally used to render 3D graphics for games and visualizations.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: From then on, AI model training and inference serving would upend the CPU’s role in the datacenter, causing widespread changes in CPU deployment and design strategies. Computing AI models requires lots of matrix multiplication, an operation that can be easily parallelized and done at massive scales on GPUs which had large arrays of vector units originally used to render 3D graphics for games and visualizations.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: From then on, AI model training and inference serving would upend the CPU’s role in the datacenter, causing widespread changes in CPU deployment and design strategies. Computing AI models requires lots of matrix multiplication, an operation that can be easily parallelized and done at massive scales on GPUs which had large arrays of vector units originally used to render 3D graphics for games and visualizations.

pro.text: 自此以后，AI 模型的训练与推理服务彻底颠覆了 CPU 在数据中心里的地位，并引发了 CPU 部署与设计策略的全面变革。AI 模型计算依赖海量的矩阵乘法，这类运算极易实现并行化，非常适合在 GPU 上大规模执行。GPU 本就配备了庞大的向量单元阵列，这些阵列最初正是为了渲染游戏与可视化 3D 图形而设计的。

“翻倍的 AVX-512 端口”带有一定的翻译腔，中文中“翻倍”通常作动词，较少直接作前置定语修饰名词。

paragraphs.source: While accelerator nodes still used host CPUs, the highly structured and relatively simple compute requirements did not take advantage of the CPU’s ability to run branchy, latency sensitive code. And with tens of vector units compared with thousands on GPUs, performance and efficiency was 100-1000x worse on CPU, especially when AI-specific GPUs added MatMul focused Tensor Cores to the mix. Despite Intel’s efforts to add more vector and matrix support with doubled AVX512 ports and dedicated AMX accelerator engines, the CPU was relegated to a support role in the datacenter. However, the internet still had to be served while power in the datacenter got prioritized to GPU compute. As a result, CPUs evolved with the times and were split into two categories.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: While accelerator nodes still used host CPUs, the highly structured and relatively simple compute requirements did not take advantage of the CPU’s ability to run branchy, latency sensitive code. And with tens of vector units compared with thousands on GPUs, performance and efficiency was 100-1000x worse on CPU, especially when AI-specific GPUs added MatMul focused Tensor Cores to the mix. Despite Intel’s efforts to add more vector and matrix support with doubled AVX512 ports and dedicated AMX accelerator engines, the CPU was relegated to a support role in the datacenter. However, the internet still had to be served while power in the datacenter got prioritized to GPU compute. As a result, CPUs evolved with the times and were split into two categories.

And with tens of vector units compared with thousands on GPUs, performance and efficiency was 100-1000x worse on CPU, especially when AI-specific GPUs added MatMul focused Tensor Cores to the mix. Despite Intel’s efforts to add more vector and matrix support with doubled AVX512 ports and dedicated AMX accelerator engines, the CPU was relegated to a support role in the datacenter. However, the internet still had to be served while power in the datacenter got prioritized to GPU compute. As a result, CPUs evolved with the times and were split into two categories.

pro.text: 尽管加速器节点依然需要宿主 CPU，但 AI 高度结构化且相对简单的计算需求，根本无法发挥 CPU 运行多分支、对延迟敏感代码的优势。此外，CPU 仅有几十个向量单元，而 GPU 动辄数千个，这导致 CPU 的性能与效率落后了 100 到 1000 倍。尤其是当专为 AI 设计的 GPU 引入了专攻矩阵乘法的张量核心 (Tensor Core) 后，差距更是悬殊。尽管英特尔试图通过翻倍的 AVX-512 端口和专用的 AMX (高级矩阵扩展) 加速引擎来强化向量与矩阵运算支持，CPU 依然在数据中心里沦为了配角。然而，互联网服务仍需维持运转，而数据中心的电力却已优先倾斜给 GPU 计算。在双重驱动下，CPU 顺应时代演进，分化成了两大阵营。

paragraphs.source: The head node CPU’s role is to manage the attached GPUs and keep them fed with data. High per-core performance with large caches and high bandwidth memory and IO are desired to keep tail latencies as low as possible. Dedicated designs such as NVIIDA’s Grace were made with coherent memory access for GPUs to utilize CPU memory as model context Key Value Cache expansions, requiring extremely high CPU to GPU bandwidth. For head nodes, 1 CPU is usually paired with 2 or 4 GPUs in each compute node. Examples include:

pro.text: 头节点 CPU 的职责是管理挂载的 GPU 并持续为其输送数据。为了尽可能压低长尾延迟，系统需要极高的单核性能、大容量缓存，以及高带宽内存与 IO。像英伟达 Grace 这样的专用设计，专门为 GPU 实现了内存一致性访问，使 GPU 能够将 CPU 内存用作模型上下文的键值缓存 (KV Cache) 扩展，这要求 CPU 与 GPU 之间具备极高的带宽。在每个计算节点中，头节点通常采用 1 颗 CPU 搭配 2 颗或 4 颗 GPU 的配置。典型例子包括：

paragraphs.source: As GPUs hogged more datacenter power budgets, the need to serve the rest of the internet as efficiently as possible accelerated the development of “Cloud-Native” CPUs. The goal is maximum throughput and requests served per socket at the best efficiency (throughput per Watt). Instead of adding more, newer CPUs to boost total throughput, old, less efficient servers are decommissioned and replaced with a far smaller number of cloud-native CPUs that met the total throughput requirement while sipping a fraction of the power, lowering operating costs and freeing up power budget for more GPU compute.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: As GPUs hogged more datacenter power budgets, the need to serve the rest of the internet as efficiently as possible accelerated the development of “Cloud-Native” CPUs. The goal is maximum throughput and requests served per socket at the best efficiency (throughput per Watt). Instead of adding more, newer CPUs to boost total throughput, old, less efficient servers are decommissioned and replaced with a far smaller number of cloud-native CPUs that met the total throughput requirement while sipping a fraction of the power, lowering operating costs and freeing up power budget for more GPU compute.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: As GPUs hogged more datacenter power budgets, the need to serve the rest of the internet as efficiently as possible accelerated the development of “Cloud-Native” CPUs. The goal is maximum throughput and requests served per socket at the best efficiency (throughput per Watt). Instead of adding more, newer CPUs to boost total throughput, old, less efficient servers are decommissioned and replaced with a far smaller number of cloud-native CPUs that met the total throughput requirement while sipping a fraction of the power, lowering operating costs and freeing up power budget for more GPU compute.

pro.text: GPU 疯狂吞噬数据中心功耗预算。为了尽可能高效地处理其余互联网业务，“云原生”CPU 的研发进程全面提速。其核心目标在于：在最优效率（每瓦吞吐量）下，实现单插槽吞吐量与请求处理量的最大化。行业不再一味堆叠新型 CPU 来拉升总吞吐量，而是果断退役低效的老旧服务器，将其替换为数量极少的云原生 CPU。这些新处理器不仅能满足总吞吐量需求，功耗更只有原先的一小部分。这不仅大幅削减了运营成本，更为扩充 GPU 算力腾出了宝贵的功耗预算。

paragraphs.source: Socket consolidation ratios of 10:1 or greater can be achieved. Millions of Intel Cascade Lake servers bought during the COVID cloud spend are being retired for the latest AMD and Intel CPUs that process at the same performance level but at less than a fifth of the power.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Socket consolidation ratios of 10:1 or greater can be achieved. Millions of Intel Cascade Lake servers bought during the COVID cloud spend are being retired for the latest AMD and Intel CPUs that process at the same performance level but at less than a fifth of the power.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Socket consolidation ratios of 10:1 or greater can be achieved. Millions of Intel Cascade Lake servers bought during the COVID cloud spend are being retired for the latest AMD and Intel CPUs that process at the same performance level but at less than a fifth of the power.

pro.text: 业界现已能实现 10:1 甚至更高的插槽整合率。疫情期间云计算投资狂潮中采购的数百万台英特尔 Cascade Lake 服务器正面临退役。取而代之的是 AMD 与英特尔最新一代 CPU，它们在提供同等性能的同时，功耗还不到老架构的五分之一。

paragraphs.source: Design wise, these Cloud-native CPUs target higher core counts with area and power efficient medium-sized cores, and have less cache and IO capabilities compared to traditional CPUs. Intel brought their Atom cores to the datacenter with Sierra Forest. AMD’s Bergamo used a more area and power efficient layout of their Zen4 core. Power efficient ARM-based designs such as AWS Graviton saw great success, while Ampere Computing targeted cloud-native compute with the Altra and AmpereOne lines.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Design wise, these Cloud-native CPUs target higher core counts with area and power efficient medium-sized cores, and have less cache and IO capabilities compared to traditional CPUs. Intel brought their Atom cores to the datacenter with Sierra Forest. AMD’s Bergamo used a more area and power efficient layout of their Zen4 core. Power efficient ARM-based designs such as AWS Graviton saw great success, while Ampere Computing targeted cloud-native compute with the Altra and AmpereOne lines.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Design wise, these Cloud-native CPUs target higher core counts with area and power efficient medium-sized cores, and have less cache and IO capabilities compared to traditional CPUs. Intel brought their Atom cores to the datacenter with Sierra Forest. AMD’s Bergamo used a more area and power efficient layout of their Zen4 core. Power efficient ARM-based designs such as AWS Graviton saw great success, while Ampere Computing targeted cloud-native compute with the Altra and AmpereOne lines.

pro.text: 架构设计上，这些云原生 CPU 采用面积与能效比高的中型核心，主攻超多核路线。相较于传统 CPU，它们适当削减了缓存容量与 IO 扩展能力。英特尔凭借 Sierra Forest 处理器，将 Atom 核心正式引入数据中心。AMD Bergamo 处理器则采用了面积与能效比更高的 Zen4 核心物理布图。具备出色能效的 ARM 架构设计（如 AWS Graviton 处理器）大获成功；Ampere Computing 也顺势推出 Altra 与 AmpereOne 系列产品线，直指云原生计算市场。

understanding.role_in_article: Highlights the current market crisis where Reinforcement Learning and Agentic AI are exhausting CPU supplies, altering vendor pricing and supply chain strategies.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Moves from the general AI accelerator boom to the acute, CPU-intensive bottlenecks caused by the RL Training Loop.
understanding.relation_to_next: Pauses the market/historical timeline to dive into the technical interconnect architectures needed to solve these exact RL bottlenecks.
key_points: Reinforcement Learning training loops require massive CPU resources, causing Frontier AI labs to exhaust x86 CPU supplies.
AI labs are now competing directly with hyperscalers for commodity servers, allowing Intel to raise prices and forcing AMD to rapidly ramp supply.

paragraphs.source: Now, CPU usage is accelerating again to support AI training and inference beyond head nodes. We can already see evidence of this in Microsoft’s “Fairwater” datacenters for OpenAI. Here, a 48MW CPU and storage building supports the main 295MW GPU cluster. This means tens of thousands of CPUs are now needed to process and manage the Petabytes of data generated by the GPUs, a use case that wouldn’t have otherwise been required without AI.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Now, CPU usage is accelerating again to support AI training and inference beyond head nodes. We can already see evidence of this in Microsoft’s “Fairwater” datacenters for OpenAI. Here, a 48MW CPU and storage building supports the main 295MW GPU cluster. This means tens of thousands of CPUs are now needed to process and manage the Petabytes of data generated by the GPUs, a use case that wouldn’t have otherwise been required without AI.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Now, CPU usage is accelerating again to support AI training and inference beyond head nodes. We can already see evidence of this in Microsoft’s “Fairwater” datacenters for OpenAI. Here, a 48MW CPU and storage building supports the main 295MW GPU cluster. This means tens of thousands of CPUs are now needed to process and manage the Petabytes of data generated by the GPUs, a use case that wouldn’t have otherwise been required without AI.

pro.text: 如今，为支持头节点之外的AI训练与推理，CPU的使用量再次激增。微软为 OpenAI 建设的 Fairwater 数据中心便是明证。在这里，一栋48兆瓦（MW）的 CPU 与存储建筑支撑着295兆瓦的主GPU集群（GPU cluster）。这意味着现在需要数以万计的 CPU 来处理和管理由 GPU 产生的 PB 级数据，如果没有 AI，这种庞大需求根本无从谈起。

paragraphs.source: The evolution of AI computing paradigms has caused this increase in CPU usage intensity. In pretraining and model fine-tuning, CPUs are used to store, shard and index data to be fed to the GPU clusters for matrix multiplication. CPUs are also used for image and video decode in multimodal models, although more fixed function media acceleration is being integrated directly into GPUs.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The evolution of AI computing paradigms has caused this increase in CPU usage intensity. In pretraining and model fine-tuning, CPUs are used to store, shard and index data to be fed to the GPU clusters for matrix multiplication. CPUs are also used for image and video decode in multimodal models, although more fixed function media acceleration is being integrated directly into GPUs.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The evolution of AI computing paradigms has caused this increase in CPU usage intensity. In pretraining and model fine-tuning, CPUs are used to store, shard and index data to be fed to the GPU clusters for matrix multiplication. CPUs are also used for image and video decode in multimodal models, although more fixed function media acceleration is being integrated directly into GPUs.

pro.text: AI 计算范式的演进直接推高了 CPU 的使用强度。在预训练与模型微调阶段，CPU 负责对数据进行存储、分片和索引，随后将其输送至 GPU 集群进行矩阵乘法运算。在多模态模型中，CPU 还被用于图像和视频解码，尽管目前已有越来越多的固定功能媒体加速模块被直接集成到 GPU 中。

paragraphs.source: Use of Reinforcement Learning techniques for model improvement increases CPU demand further. From our deep dive on Reinforcement Learning, we see that in an RL training loop, the “RL Environment” needs to execute the actions generated by the model and calculate the appropriate reward. To do this in areas such as coding and mathematics, lots of CPUs are needed in parallel to perform code compilation, verification, interpretation, and tool use. CPUs are also heavily involved in complex physics simulations and verifying generated synthetic data at high precision. The growing complexity of RL environments needed to scale models further thus necessitates large high-performance CPU clusters located close to the main GPU clusters to keep them busy and minimize GPU idle time.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Use of Reinforcement Learning techniques for model improvement increases CPU demand further. From our deep dive on Reinforcement Learning, we see that in an RL training loop, the “RL Environment” needs to execute the actions generated by the model and calculate the appropriate reward. To do this in areas such as coding and mathematics, lots of CPUs are needed in parallel to perform code compilation, verification, interpretation, and tool use. CPUs are also heavily involved in complex physics simulations and verifying generated synthetic data at high precision. The growing complexity of RL environments needed to scale models further thus necessitates large high-performance CPU clusters located close to the main GPU clusters to keep them busy and minimize GPU idle time. This increasing reliance on RL and CPUs in the training loop is creating a new bottleneck, as AI accelerators are improving in Performance per Watt at a far greater rate than CPUs, meaning a future GPU generation such as Rubin may require an even higher ratio of CPU to GPU power than the 1:6 ratio seen in Fairwater above.

pro.text: 采用强化学习 (RL) 技术来改进模型，进一步推高了 CPU 需求。正如我们在强化学习 (RL) 深度分析中所指出的，在 RL 训练循环中，“RL环境”需要执行模型生成的动作并计算相应的奖励。在编程和数学等领域，这要求大量 CPU 并行工作，以完成代码编译、验证、解释及工具调用。此外，CPU 还深度参与复杂的物理模拟，并对生成的合成数据进行高精度验证。为进一步扩展模型规模，RL环境日益复杂。这就要求在主 GPU 集群附近部署大型高性能 CPU 集群，以确保 GPU 满载运行，最大限度减少 GPU 空闲时间。

paragraphs.source: On the inference side, the rise of Retrieval Augmented Generative (RAG) models that search and use the internet along with agentic models that invoke tools and query databases has significantly increased the need for general-purpose CPU compute to service these requests. With the ability to send out API calls to multiple sources, each agent can essentially use the internet far more intensively than a human can by doing simple Google searches. AWS and Azure are doing massive CPU buildouts of their own Graviton and Cobalt lines of CPUs as well as purchasing even more x86 general purpose servers for this stepfold increase in internet traffic.

pro.text: 在推理端，能够搜索并利用互联网的检索增强生成 (RAG) 模型日益兴起，加之能够调用工具和查询数据库的智能体模型不断涌现，双重驱动下，处理这些请求所需的通用 CPU 算力需求大幅飙升。由于具备向多个数据源发送 API 调用的能力，每个智能体对互联网的使用强度，本质上远超人类进行简单的谷歌搜索。为应对互联网流量的阶梯式跃升，AWS 和 Azure 正在大规模部署其自研的 Graviton 和 Cobalt 系列 CPU，同时采购更多的商用 x86 通用服务器。

paragraphs.source: As we go through 2026, the demands on datacenter CPU and DRAM are only getting stronger. Frontier AI labs are running out of CPUs for their RL Training needs and are scrambling for CPU allocation by competing directly with the cloud providers for commodity x86 CPU servers. Intel, facing the unexpected depletion of their CPU inventory, is looking to raise prices across their Xeon line while they ramp additional tools to shore up CPU production. AMD has been increasing their supply capability to grow and take share in a server CPU TAM it believes will grow in the “strong double digits” in 2026. We will discuss how the CPU landscape evolves beyond 2026 for our subscribers below.

Frontier AI labs are running out of CPUs for their RL Training needs and are scrambling for CPU allocation by competing directly with the cloud providers for commodity x86 CPU servers. Intel, facing the unexpected depletion of their CPU inventory, is looking to raise prices across their Xeon line while they ramp additional tools to shore up CPU production. AMD has been increasing their supply capability to grow and take share in a server CPU TAM it believes will grow in the “strong double digits” in 2026. We will discuss how the CPU landscape evolves beyond 2026 for our subscribers below.

AMD has been increasing their supply capability to grow and take share in a server CPU TAM it believes will grow in the “strong double digits” in 2026. We will discuss how the CPU landscape evolves beyond 2026 for our subscribers below.

pro.text: 步入 2026 年，数据中心对 CPU 和 DRAM 的需求只会愈发强劲。前沿 AI 实验室的 CPU 已经无法满足其 RL 训练需求，正直接与云服务提供商抢夺商用 x86 CPU 服务器，疯狂争夺 CPU 供货配额。面对 CPU 库存意外告罄的局面，英特尔正计划全面上调其至强系列的价格，同时增加生产设备以提振 CPU 产能。AMD 则一直在提升供货能力，旨在实现增长并抢占服务器 CPU 总潜在市场 (TAM) 份额，其笃定该市场在 2026 年将实现“强劲的双位数”增长。接下来，我们将为订阅者深入探讨 2026 年之后 CPU 市场格局的演变。

paragraphs.source: To appreciate the design changes and philosophies of the 2026 CPUs, we have to understand how multi-core CPUs work and the evolution of interconnects as core counts grew. With multiple cores comes the need to connect those cores together. Early dual-core designs such as Intel’s Pentium D and Xeon Paxville in 2005 simply consisted of two independent single cores, with core-to-core communication done off-package via the Front Side Bus (FSB) to a Northbridge chip that also housed the memory controllers.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: To appreciate the design changes and philosophies of the 2026 CPUs, we have to understand how multi-core CPUs work and the evolution of interconnects as core counts grew. With multiple cores comes the need to connect those cores together. Early dual-core designs such as Intel’s Pentium D and Xeon Paxville in 2005 simply consisted of two independent single cores, with core-to-core communication done off-package via the Front Side Bus (FSB) to a Northbridge chip that also housed the memory controllers. AMD’s Athlon 64 X2, also in 2005, could be considered a true dual-core processor with two cores and an integrated memory controller (IMC) on the same die, allowing the cores to communicate with each other and to memory and IO controllers directly within the silicon through on-die NoC (Network on Chip) data fabrics.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: To appreciate the design changes and philosophies of the 2026 CPUs, we have to understand how multi-core CPUs work and the evolution of interconnects as core counts grew. With multiple cores comes the need to connect those cores together. Early dual-core designs such as Intel’s Pentium D and Xeon Paxville in 2005 simply consisted of two independent single cores, with core-to-core communication done off-package via the Front Side Bus (FSB) to a Northbridge chip that also housed the memory controllers. AMD’s Athlon 64 X2, also in 2005, could be considered a true dual-core processor with two cores and an integrated memory controller (IMC) on the same die, allowing the cores to communicate with each other and to memory and IO controllers directly within the silicon through on-die NoC (Network on Chip) data fabrics.

pro.text: 要理解 2026 年 CPU 的设计变革与理念，必须先弄清多核 CPU 的工作原理，以及互连架构随核心数量增长的演进历程。既然有多个核心，自然需要将它们连接起来。2005 年问世的早期双核设计（如英特尔的奔腾 D 和至强 Paxville）仅仅是两个独立的单核。核心间通信必须在封装外部进行，数据通过前端总线 (FSB) 传至北桥芯片，而北桥芯片同时也搭载了内存控制器。

applied_terms: Hardware terminology must be strictly accurate: 'Interconnects' (互连架构), 'Crossbar' (交叉开关), 'Ring' (环形互连), 'Mesh' (网格互连).
Avoid translating 'core' as anything other than '核心' in this context.

understanding.role_in_article: Shifts the focus from market history to deep technical architecture, explaining how cores communicate on a die.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Uses the massive compute demands established in the RL era to justify a deep dive into the physical limitations of core scaling.
understanding.relation_to_next: Lays the mandatory technical groundwork for the upcoming vendor-by-vendor breakdown of 2026+ CPU architectures.
key_points: Understanding the future of datacenter CPUs requires a deep understanding of multi-core interconnect topologies.
As core counts explode to meet AI demands, the evolution from Crossbar to Ring to Mesh interconnects becomes the primary architectural bottleneck.

原文的“grow”是描述技术长期演进的客观中性词，译文使用了“飙升”一词，在描述跨越十余年的技术发展史时显得情绪色彩过重（Tone inflation）。

paragraphs.source: AMD’s Athlon 64 X2, also in 2005, could be considered a true dual-core processor with two cores and an integrated memory controller (IMC) on the same die, allowing the cores to communicate with each other and to memory and IO controllers directly within the silicon through on-die NoC (Network on Chip) data fabrics.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: To appreciate the design changes and philosophies of the 2026 CPUs, we have to understand how multi-core CPUs work and the evolution of interconnects as core counts grew. With multiple cores comes the need to connect those cores together. Early dual-core designs such as Intel’s Pentium D and Xeon Paxville in 2005 simply consisted of two independent single cores, with core-to-core communication done off-package via the Front Side Bus (FSB) to a Northbridge chip that also housed the memory controllers. AMD’s Athlon 64 X2, also in 2005, could be considered a true dual-core processor with two cores and an integrated memory controller (IMC) on the same die, allowing the cores to communicate with each other and to memory and IO controllers directly within the silicon through on-die NoC (Network on Chip) data fabrics.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: To appreciate the design changes and philosophies of the 2026 CPUs, we have to understand how multi-core CPUs work and the evolution of interconnects as core counts grew. With multiple cores comes the need to connect those cores together. Early dual-core designs such as Intel’s Pentium D and Xeon Paxville in 2005 simply consisted of two independent single cores, with core-to-core communication done off-package via the Front Side Bus (FSB) to a Northbridge chip that also housed the memory controllers. AMD’s Athlon 64 X2, also in 2005, could be considered a true dual-core processor with two cores and an integrated memory controller (IMC) on the same die, allowing the cores to communicate with each other and to memory and IO controllers directly within the silicon through on-die NoC (Network on Chip) data fabrics.

pro.text: 同样在 2005 年推出的 AMD 速龙 64 X2 则算得上是真正的双核处理器。它将两个核心与集成内存控制器 (IMC) 集成在了同一块裸片上。这使得核心之间、核心与内存及 IO 控制器之间，能够通过片上 NoC（片上网络）数据网络直接在芯片内部进行通信。

paragraphs.source: Intel’s subsequent Tulsa generation included 16MB of L3 cache shared between the two cores and functions as an on-die core to core data fabric. As we will see later, these on-die data fabrics will become a crucial factor in datacenter CPU design as core counts grow in the hundreds.

paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Intel’s subsequent Tulsa generation included 16MB of L3 cache shared between the two cores and functions as an on-die core to core data fabric. As we will see later, these on-die data fabrics will become a crucial factor in datacenter CPU design as core counts grow in the hundreds.

pro.text: 英特尔随后的 Tulsa 处理器配备了由两个核心共享的 16MB 三级缓存，该缓存同时充当片上的核心间数据网络。后文将会看到，随着核心数量飙升至数百个，这些片上数据网络将成为数据中心 CPU 设计的关键因素。

paragraphs.source: As designers tried to increase core counts further, they ran into scaling limits of these early interconnects. As minimal latency and uniformity was desired, crossbar designs were used in an all-to-all fashion, where every core has a discrete link to all other cores on die. However, the number of links increased greatly with more cores, increasing complexity.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: As designers tried to increase core counts further, they ran into scaling limits of these early interconnects. As minimal latency and uniformity was desired, crossbar designs were used in an all-to-all fashion, where every core has a discrete link to all other cores on die. However, the number of links increased greatly with more cores, increasing complexity.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: As designers tried to increase core counts further, they ran into scaling limits of these early interconnects. As minimal latency and uniformity was desired, crossbar designs were used in an all-to-all fashion, where every core has a discrete link to all other cores on die. However, the number of links increased greatly with more cores, increasing complexity.

pro.text: 随着设计人员试图进一步增加核心数量，他们触及了这些早期互连架构的扩展瓶颈。由于追求极低延迟与一致性，交叉开关（Crossbar）设计采用了全互连模式，即裸片上的每个核心都有一条独立链路与其他所有核心相连。然而，随着核心数量增多，链路数量激增，导致复杂性大幅上升。

paragraphs.source: The practical limit for most designs ended at 4 cores, with higher core count processors achieved with multi-chip modules and dual-core modules that shared and L2 cache and data fabric socket between core pairs. The crossbar wiring is usually done in the metal lines above the shared L3 caches, saving area. Intel’s 6-core Dunnington in 2008 used three dual-core modules with 16MB of shared L3.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The practical limit for most designs ended at 4 cores, with higher core count processors achieved with multi-chip modules and dual-core modules that shared and L2 cache and data fabric socket between core pairs. The crossbar wiring is usually done in the metal lines above the shared L3 caches, saving area. Intel’s 6-core Dunnington in 2008 used three dual-core modules with 16MB of shared L3.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The practical limit for most designs ended at 4 cores, with higher core count processors achieved with multi-chip modules and dual-core modules that shared and L2 cache and data fabric socket between core pairs. The crossbar wiring is usually done in the metal lines above the shared L3 caches, saving area. Intel’s 6-core Dunnington in 2008 used three dual-core modules with 16MB of shared L3.

pro.text: 大多数设计的实际极限止步于 4 个核心，核心数更高的处理器则是通过多芯片模块和双核模块来实现的，这些模块在核心对之间共享L2缓存与数据网络端口。交叉开关布线通常在共享L3缓存上方的金属层中完成，以节省面积。2008 年问世的英特尔 6 核 Dunnington 处理器便采用了三个双核模块，并配备 16MB 共享L3缓存。

issues.description: “Home agents” 在计算机体系结构（特别是英特尔的 NUMA/一致性协议中）的标准译法是“归属代理”或“主节点代理”。译为“主代理”不够精确，容易引起歧义。
“counter rotating” 意为两个环的数据传输方向相反。此处译为“双向反转环”略显生硬且容易引发误解。此外，在第11段中同词被译为“反向旋转”，前后术语不一致。
“Non Uniform Memory Access” 的业界标准中文术语是“非一致性内存访问”或“非均匀内存访问”，“非统一内存访问”不符合国内硬件工程师的惯用表达。
译文“L3缓存切片”中，英文字母与中文字符之间缺少空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。此问题在第3段和第13段中也有出现。

issues.description: “Home agents” 在计算机体系结构（特别是英特尔的 NUMA/一致性协议中）的标准译法是“归属代理”或“主节点代理”。译为“主代理”不够精确，容易引起歧义。
“counter rotating” 意为两个环的数据传输方向相反。此处译为“双向反转环”略显生硬且容易引发误解。此外，在第11段中同词被译为“反向旋转”，前后术语不一致。
“Non Uniform Memory Access” 的业界标准中文术语是“非一致性内存访问”或“非均匀内存访问”，“非统一内存访问”不符合国内硬件工程师的惯用表达。
译文“L3缓存切片”中，英文字母与中文字符之间缺少空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。此问题在第3段和第13段中也有出现。

paragraphs.source: To scale past this limit, Intel implemented a ring bus architecture with their Nehalem-EX (Beckton) Xeons in 2010, bringing 8 cores with integrated memory controllers and inter-socket QPI links into a single die. Implemented in earlier years within ATi Radeon GPUs and the IBM Cell processor, the ring bus arranges all nodes into a loop, with ring stops integrated into the L3 cache slices and wiring in the metal layers above the cache. Caching and Home agents deal with memory snooping between cores and coherence with the memory controller.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: To scale past this limit, Intel implemented a ring bus architecture with their Nehalem-EX (Beckton) Xeons in 2010, bringing 8 cores with integrated memory controllers and inter-socket QPI links into a single die. Implemented in earlier years within ATi Radeon GPUs and the IBM Cell processor, the ring bus arranges all nodes into a loop, with ring stops integrated into the L3 cache slices and wiring in the metal layers above the cache. Caching and Home agents deal with memory snooping between cores and coherence with the memory controller.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: To scale past this limit, Intel implemented a ring bus architecture with their Nehalem-EX (Beckton) Xeons in 2010, bringing 8 cores with integrated memory controllers and inter-socket QPI links into a single die. Implemented in earlier years within ATi Radeon GPUs and the IBM Cell processor, the ring bus arranges all nodes into a loop, with ring stops integrated into the L3 cache slices and wiring in the metal layers above the cache. Caching and Home agents deal with memory snooping between cores and coherence with the memory controller.

pro.text: 为了突破这一极限，英特尔于 2010 年在其 Nehalem-EX (Beckton) 至强处理器中引入了环形总线架构，将 8 个核心、集成内存控制器以及跨插槽 QPI 链路整合到了单一裸片中。环形总线早年曾应用于 ATi Radeon GPU 与 IBM Cell 处理器中。该架构将所有节点排布成一个环路，环形站点直接集成在 L3 缓存切片内，布线则走缓存上方的金属层。缓存与归属代理负责处理核心间的内存嗅探，并维护与内存控制器的一致性。

issues.description: “Home agents” 在计算机体系结构（特别是英特尔的 NUMA/一致性协议中）的标准译法是“归属代理”或“主节点代理”。译为“主代理”不够精确，容易引起歧义。
“counter rotating” 意为两个环的数据传输方向相反。此处译为“双向反转环”略显生硬且容易引发误解。此外，在第11段中同词被译为“反向旋转”，前后术语不一致。
“Non Uniform Memory Access” 的业界标准中文术语是“非一致性内存访问”或“非均匀内存访问”，“非统一内存访问”不符合国内硬件工程师的惯用表达。
译文“L3缓存切片”中，英文字母与中文字符之间缺少空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。此问题在第3段和第13段中也有出现。

paragraphs.source: Data from each ring stop’s core and L3 cache slice is queued and injected into the ring, with data advancing one stop per clock to its target destination. This means core to core access latency is no longer uniform, with cores on opposite sides of the ring having to wait additional cycles compared to directly adjacent cores. To help with latency and congestion, two counter rotating rings are implemented, with the optimal direction of travel chosen based on address and ring loading. With wiring complexity now moderated, Intel could scale core counts to 8 on Nehalem-EX and 10 for Westmere-EX. However, scaling beyond that with a single ring would lead to problems with coherence and latency as the ring gets too long.

This means core to core access latency is no longer uniform, with cores on opposite sides of the ring having to wait additional cycles compared to directly adjacent cores. To help with latency and congestion, two counter rotating rings are implemented, with the optimal direction of travel chosen based on address and ring loading. With wiring complexity now moderated, Intel could scale core counts to 8 on Nehalem-EX and 10 for Westmere-EX. However, scaling beyond that with a single ring would lead to problems with coherence and latency as the ring gets too long.

pro.text: 来自每个环形站点的核心与 L3缓存切片的数据经排队后注入环路，数据每个时钟周期向前推进一个站点，直至抵达目标位置。这意味着核心间的访问延迟不再均衡：相比紧邻的核心，位于环路相对两侧的核心需要等待更多周期。为缓解延迟与拥堵，设计中引入了两个反向旋转的环路，并根据地址与环路负载选择最优传输方向。随着布线复杂度得到有效控制，英特尔得以将 Nehalem-EX 的核心数扩展至 8 个，并在 Westmere-EX 上进一步增至 10 个。然而，若继续沿用单环架构进一步扩充核心，过长的环路必将引发一致性与延迟问题。

paragraphs.source: To scale core count to 15 for the Ivy Bridge generation, Intel had to get clever with the routing topology. The cores are arranged in three columns of five, with three ‘virtual rings’ looping around the columns. Switches in the ring stops controlled the direction of travel along the half rings, creating a “virtual” triple ring configuration.

The cores are arranged in three columns of five, with three ‘virtual rings’ looping around the columns. Switches in the ring stops controlled the direction of travel along the half rings, creating a “virtual” triple ring configuration.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: To scale core count to 15 for the Ivy Bridge generation, Intel had to get clever with the routing topology. The cores are arranged in three columns of five, with three ‘virtual rings’ looping around the columns. Switches in the ring stops controlled the direction of travel along the half rings, creating a “virtual” triple ring configuration.

pro.text: 为了在 Ivy Bridge 世代将核心数扩展至 15 个，英特尔必须在路由拓扑上巧妙布局。核心被排布为 3 列（每列 5 个），并由 3 个“虚拟环”环绕这三列核心。环形站点内的交换机负责控制数据在半环上的传输方向，由此构建出一种“虚拟”的三环架构。

paragraphs.source: In 2014, Intel changed topologies yet again with the 18-core Haswell HCC die featuring dual independent counter rotating ring buses connected with a pair of bi-directional buffered switches. Memory controllers were split amongst the two rings, with the 8-core ring also housing the IO ring stops. The MCC die variant wrapped a single half-ring back on itself. Broadwell HCC, released in 2015, brought core counts up to 24 with dual 12-core ring buses.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: In 2014, Intel changed topologies yet again with the 18-core Haswell HCC die featuring dual independent counter rotating ring buses connected with a pair of bi-directional buffered switches. Memory controllers were split amongst the two rings, with the 8-core ring also housing the IO ring stops. The MCC die variant wrapped a single half-ring back on itself. Broadwell HCC, released in 2015, brought core counts up to 24 with dual 12-core ring buses.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: In 2014, Intel changed topologies yet again with the 18-core Haswell HCC die featuring dual independent counter rotating ring buses connected with a pair of bi-directional buffered switches. Memory controllers were split amongst the two rings, with the 8-core ring also housing the IO ring stops. The MCC die variant wrapped a single half-ring back on itself. Broadwell HCC, released in 2015, brought core counts up to 24 with dual 12-core ring buses.

pro.text: 2014 年，英特尔在 18 核的 Haswell 高核心数 (HCC) 裸片上再次更改了拓扑结构，引入了双独立反向旋转环形总线，两者通过一对双向缓冲交换机相连。内存控制器被分置于两个环形总线中，其中 8 核的环形总线还容纳了 IO 环形站点。中等核心数 (MCC) 裸片变体则将单个半环自身折返相连。2015 年发布的 Broadwell HCC 将核心数提升至 24 个，配备了双 12 核环形总线。

paragraphs.source: The downside to stitching multiple rings together is the increased variability in core to core and memory access latency, especially so when cores on one ring are accessing the memory of the other ring. This Non Uniform Memory Access (NUMA) was detrimental to system performance for programs that are latency sensitive with high core to core interactivity.

This Non Uniform Memory Access (NUMA) was detrimental to system performance for programs that are latency sensitive with high core to core interactivity.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The downside to stitching multiple rings together is the increased variability in core to core and memory access latency, especially so when cores on one ring are accessing the memory of the other ring. This Non Uniform Memory Access (NUMA) was detrimental to system performance for programs that are latency sensitive with high core to core interactivity.

pro.text: 将多个环形总线拼接在一起的缺点在于，核心间通信与内存访问的延迟差异增大，当一个环上的核心访问另一个环的内存时尤为明显。对于那些对延迟敏感且核心间交互频繁的程序而言，这种非均匀内存访问 (NUMA) 对系统性能极为不利。

issues.description: “Home agents” 在计算机体系结构（特别是英特尔的 NUMA/一致性协议中）的标准译法是“归属代理”或“主节点代理”。译为“主代理”不够精确，容易引起歧义。
“counter rotating” 意为两个环的数据传输方向相反。此处译为“双向反转环”略显生硬且容易引发误解。此外，在第11段中同词被译为“反向旋转”，前后术语不一致。
“Non Uniform Memory Access” 的业界标准中文术语是“非一致性内存访问”或“非均匀内存访问”，“非统一内存访问”不符合国内硬件工程师的惯用表达。
译文“L3缓存切片”中，英文字母与中文字符之间缺少空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。此问题在第3段和第13段中也有出现。

paragraphs.source: To help with this, Intel offered a “Cluster on Die” configuration option in the BIOS that treated the two rings as independent processors. The operating system would show the CPU being split into two NUMA nodes, each with direct access to half the local memory and L3 cache. Testing in CoD mode showed that latency within each ring stayed under 50ns while access to the other ring took over 100ns, illustrating the latency penalty of going through the buffered switches.

inline_elements.text: Testing in CoD mode

paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: To help with this, Intel offered a “Cluster on Die” configuration option in the BIOS that treated the two rings as independent processors. The operating system would show the CPU being split into two NUMA nodes, each with direct access to half the local memory and L3 cache. [Testing in CoD mode](https://old.chipsandcheese.com/2023/11/07/core-to-core-latency-data-on-large-systems/) showed that latency within each ring stayed under 50ns while access to the other ring took over 100ns, illustrating the latency penalty of going through the buffered switches.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: To help with this, Intel offered a “Cluster on Die” configuration option in the BIOS that treated the two rings as independent processors. The operating system would show the CPU being split into two NUMA nodes, each with direct access to half the local memory and L3 cache. Testing in CoD mode showed that latency within each ring stayed under 50ns while access to the other ring took over 100ns, illustrating the latency penalty of going through the buffered switches.

parent_inline_elements.text: Testing in CoD mode

pro.text: 为了缓解这一问题，英特尔在 BIOS 中提供了一个“片上集群 (CoD)”配置选项，将两个环形总线视为独立的处理器。操作系统会显示 CPU 被拆分为两个 NUMA 节点，每个节点可直接访问一半的本地内存和 L3缓存。在 CoD 模式下的测试表明，每个环内的延迟保持在 50ns 以下，而跨环访问则超过 100ns，这凸显了经过缓冲交换机所带来的延迟惩罚。

paragraphs.source: While these methods helped Intel increase core counts to 24, it was not an elegant nor scalable solution. Adding a third ring and two more sets of buffered switches would be too complicated and impractical, creating many NUMA clusters. A new interconnect architecture was required for more cores.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: While these methods helped Intel increase core counts to 24, it was not an elegant nor scalable solution. Adding a third ring and two more sets of buffered switches would be too complicated and impractical, creating many NUMA clusters. A new interconnect architecture was required for more cores.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: While these methods helped Intel increase core counts to 24, it was not an elegant nor scalable solution. Adding a third ring and two more sets of buffered switches would be too complicated and impractical, creating many NUMA clusters. A new interconnect architecture was required for more cores.

pro.text: 虽然这些方法帮助英特尔将核心数增加到了 24 个，但这既不优雅，也缺乏可扩展性。增加第三个环形总线和另外两组缓冲交换机会过于复杂且不切实际，还会产生过多的 NUMA 集群。要支持更多核心，必须采用全新的互连架构。

paragraphs.source: To solve the scalability problem, Intel adopted the mesh interconnect architecture used in their 2016 Xeon Phi “Knights Landing” processor for their mainline Skylake-X Xeon Scalable CPUs in 2017, bringing 28 cores in the XCC die. While core counts did not increase much over Broadwell, the design would form the base that would scale core counts over the next decade.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: To solve the scalability problem, Intel adopted the mesh interconnect architecture used in their 2016 Xeon Phi “Knights Landing” processor for their mainline Skylake-X Xeon Scalable CPUs in 2017, bringing 28 cores in the XCC die. While core counts did not increase much over Broadwell, the design would form the base that would scale core counts over the next decade.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: To solve the scalability problem, Intel adopted the mesh interconnect architecture used in their 2016 Xeon Phi “Knights Landing” processor for their mainline Skylake-X Xeon Scalable CPUs in 2017, bringing 28 cores in the XCC die. While core counts did not increase much over Broadwell, the design would form the base that would scale core counts over the next decade.

pro.text: 为了解决可扩展性问题，英特尔在 2017 年将其 2016 款至强 Phi “Knights Landing” 处理器中使用的网格互连架构，引入到主流的 Skylake-X 至强可扩展 CPU 中，在 XCC (极高核心数) 裸片中集成了 28 个核心。尽管核心数相比 Broadwell 并未大幅增加，但这一设计奠定了未来十年持续扩展核心数的基础。

paragraphs.source: In a mesh architecture, cores are arranged in a grid, with each column and row connected with half rings, forming a 2D mesh array. Each mesh stop can house cores and L3 cache slices, PCIe IO, the IMC, and accelerators. Routing between cores is done in a circular manner, with data travelling in the vertical direction before moving horizontally across. The caching and home agents are now distributed across all the ring stops along with their snoop filters for memory coherence across the network.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: In a mesh architecture, cores are arranged in a grid, with each column and row connected with half rings, forming a 2D mesh array. Each mesh stop can house cores and L3 cache slices, PCIe IO, the IMC, and accelerators. Routing between cores is done in a circular manner, with data travelling in the vertical direction before moving horizontally across. The caching and home agents are now distributed across all the ring stops along with their snoop filters for memory coherence across the network.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: In a mesh architecture, cores are arranged in a grid, with each column and row connected with half rings, forming a 2D mesh array. Each mesh stop can house cores and L3 cache slices, PCIe IO, the IMC, and accelerators. Routing between cores is done in a circular manner, with data travelling in the vertical direction before moving horizontally across. The caching and home agents are now distributed across all the ring stops along with their snoop filters for memory coherence across the network.

pro.text: 在网格架构中，核心呈网格状排列，每行每列通过半环相连，形成 2D 网格阵列。每个网格节点可容纳核心与 L3缓存切片、PCIe IO、IMC 以及加速器。核心间的路由采用环形机制，数据先沿垂直方向传输，再沿水平方向移动。缓存与主代理及其嗅探过滤器如今分布在所有环形节点上，以维持整个网络内的内存一致性。

paragraphs.source: With a mesh network and multiple memory controllers on opposite sides of the die, memory access and core to core latency would vary significantly with large meshes. As with the earlier Cluster on Die approach, several clustering modes were offered that split the mesh into quadrants for Sub-NUMA Clustering (SNC), reducing average latencies at the expense of treating each processor as multiple sockets with smaller L3 and memory access pools for each NUMA node.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: With a mesh network and multiple memory controllers on opposite sides of the die, memory access and core to core latency would vary significantly with large meshes. As with the earlier Cluster on Die approach, several clustering modes were offered that split the mesh into quadrants for Sub-NUMA Clustering (SNC), reducing average latencies at the expense of treating each processor as multiple sockets with smaller L3 and memory access pools for each NUMA node.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: With a mesh network and multiple memory controllers on opposite sides of the die, memory access and core to core latency would vary significantly with large meshes. As with the earlier Cluster on Die approach, several clustering modes were offered that split the mesh into quadrants for Sub-NUMA Clustering (SNC), reducing average latencies at the expense of treating each processor as multiple sockets with smaller L3 and memory access pools for each NUMA node.

pro.text: 在网格网络中，由于多个内存控制器分布在裸片两侧，大型网格的内存访问与核心间延迟差异极大。与早期的片上集群 (CoD) 方法类似，系统提供了几种集群模式，将网格划分为四个象限以实现子NUMA集群 (SNC)。这降低了平均延迟，但代价是将每个处理器视为多个插槽，且每个 NUMA 节点的 L3缓存与内存访问池也相应缩小。

paragraphs.source: In Knights Landing, each mesh stop housed two cores with a shared L2 cache. The mesh grid is 6 columns by 9 rows in size, with top and bottom rows more IO and MCDRAM. The mesh network runs on it’s own clock, and can dynamically adjust mesh clocks to save power. On Knights Landing, the mesh ran at 1.6GHz.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: In Knights Landing, each mesh stop housed two cores with a shared L2 cache. The mesh grid is 6 columns by 9 rows in size, with top and bottom rows more IO and MCDRAM. The mesh network runs on it’s own clock, and can dynamically adjust mesh clocks to save power. On Knights Landing, the mesh ran at 1.6GHz.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: In Knights Landing, each mesh stop housed two cores with a shared L2 cache. The mesh grid is 6 columns by 9 rows in size, with top and bottom rows more IO and MCDRAM. The mesh network runs on it’s own clock, and can dynamically adjust mesh clocks to save power. On Knights Landing, the mesh ran at 1.6GHz.

pro.text: 在 Knights Landing 中，每个网格节点容纳两个核心，并共享 L2缓存。网格阵列规模为 6 列 9 行，顶部和底部行配置了更多的 IO 和 MCDRAM。网格网络运行在独立的时钟频率上，并能动态调整网格时钟以节省功耗。在 Knights Landing 上，网格运行频率为 1.6GHz。

paragraphs.source: With Skylake-X, the 28 cores are arranged in a 6x6 mesh with a north IO cap and 2 spots for the IMC on the sides. The mesh array is smaller due to the size of the cores, which added more L2 cache and an AVX-512 extension to the core for increased floating point performance. The die size would exceed the 26 x 33 mm reticle limit if another row or column were to be added. With a smaller mesh and higher CPU frequencies of up to 4.5GHz, the mesh clock was increased to 2.4GHz, allowing similar average latencies to Broadwell’s dual rings.

The die size would exceed the 26 x 33 mm reticle limit if another row or column were to be added. With a smaller mesh and higher CPU frequencies of up to 4.5GHz, the mesh clock was increased to 2.4GHz, allowing similar average latencies to Broadwell’s dual rings.

pro.text: 在 Skylake-X 中，28 个核心排列成 6x6 网格阵列，顶部设有北侧 IO 模块，两侧预留了两个 IMC (集成内存控制器) 位置。核心体积增大导致网格阵列规模缩小。为提升浮点性能，这些核心内部增加了更多的 L2缓存与 AVX-512 扩展。若再增加一行或一列，裸片尺寸必将突破 26 x 33 毫米的光刻掩模版极限。得益于网格规模缩小以及 CPU 频率提升（最高达 4.5GHz），网格时钟频率也随之拉高至 2.4GHz。这使得其平均延迟与 Broadwell 的双环架构不相上下。

pro.text: 随后的 Cascade Lake 与 Cooper Lake 处理器仅作微调，依然沿用相同的 28 核心布局。顺带一提，面对 AMD 携 EPYC 杀回数据中心市场，英特尔在 Cascade Lake-AP 上推出了 56 核心的双裸片 MCM (多芯片模块)，但随后又砍掉了 Cooper Lake CPX-4 的类似版本。

paragraphs.source: The next Ice Lake generation benefited from a node shrink from 14nm to 10nm, allowing core counts to increase to 40 cores in a 8x7 mesh, the maximum within the reticle limit. However, the next generation Sapphire Rapids was still going to be on the same node and with more features. That placed Intel in a pickle with how to increase core counts again.

However, the next generation Sapphire Rapids was still going to be on the same node and with more features. That placed Intel in a pickle with how to increase core counts again.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The next Ice Lake generation benefited from a node shrink from 14nm to 10nm, allowing core counts to increase to 40 cores in a 8x7 mesh, the maximum within the reticle limit. However, the next generation Sapphire Rapids was still going to be on the same node and with more features. That placed Intel in a pickle with how to increase core counts again.

pro.text: 下一代 Ice Lake 受益于制程节点从 14nm 演进至 10nm，核心数得以提升至 40 个，呈 8x7 网格排列。这已是光刻掩模版极限下的最大规模。然而，紧随其后的下一代 Sapphire Rapids 仍将停留在同一制程节点，且需集成更多功能特性。这让英特尔在如何继续堆叠核心数上进退维谷。
